description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-rk33
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T19:41:28Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
Map pins disappear after clicking several restaurants. Caused by React re-render thrashing in `@vis.gl/react-google-maps` AdvancedMarker components and an unstable `handleMapClick` callback with `[props]` dependency array (props is a new object every render, so useCallback never memoizes).

## Key Files
- src/components/MapView.tsx — marker rendering, handleMapClick (line ~186-188)
- src/components/HappyHourApp.tsx — state management, venue selection

## Fix Approach
1. Replace `[props]` dependency in `handleMapClick` useCallback with specific props
2. Memoize marker components or use stable keys
3. Ensure AdvancedMarker lifecycle is stable across re-renders
4. Reference working CRA version: commit ed5ebc856051f9340e7229a72fd06da9194bca64

## Acceptance Criteria
- Click 20+ venues rapidly — no pins disappear
- No console errors during rapid clicking
- Map markers maintain correct opacity states
title: Fix map pin disappearing on rapid venue clicks

close_reason: Merged to main: 'fix: add Referer header to Google Places API calls to bypass HTTP referrer restriction'
description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-08hv
attached_at: 2026-02-23T21:44:44Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Venues added via Deal Updater save to Supabase but lat/lng are null, so they don't appear on the map (MapView.tsx:92 filters on v.latitude != null && v.longitude != null).

Root cause: /src/app/api/venues/route.ts uses process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY for server-side Google Places API fetch calls (Text Search + Place Details). This key has HTTP referrer restrictions in Google Cloud Console — it expects a browser Referer header. Node.js server-side fetch sends no Referer, so Google silently returns REQUEST_DENIED and the catch block eats the error, leaving lat/lng null.

Fix: Add Referer header to both fetch calls in the POST handler at /src/app/api/venues/route.ts. The Vercel production URL for this app is https://socializers-happyhour-frontend.vercel.app/ — add this as the Referer header to both:
1. Text Search fetch: https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/textsearch/json?...
2. Place Details fetch: https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/details/json?...

Change:
  const textSearchRes = await fetch(`https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/textsearch/json?query=${query}&key=${apiKey}`);

To:
  const textSearchRes = await fetch(`https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/textsearch/json?query=${query}&key=${apiKey}`, { headers: { Referer: 'https://socializers-happyhour-frontend.vercel.app/' } });

And same for the Place Details fetch call.

The file path is: src/app/api/venues/route.ts (in the rig).

After making the change, deploy to Vercel: pnpm run build should succeed (no new deps needed).

Work on a feature branch, push, run gt done. Do NOT push to main directly.
title: Fix geocoding: venues save without lat/lng, don't appear on map

description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-psef
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T19:46:18Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
LockManager timeout errors in console. Causes: (1) Header.tsx contains dead onAuthStateChange listener (file not imported anywhere), (2) UserMenu.tsx creates fresh Supabase client on mount, (3) createBrowserClient() in supabase-browser.ts not singleton.

## Key Files
- src/components/UserMenu.tsx — active auth listener, creates client in useEffect
- src/components/Header.tsx — DEAD CODE with redundant auth listener (DELETE THIS FILE)
- src/lib/supabase-browser.ts — createClient() not singleton

## Fix Approach
1. DELETE src/components/Header.tsx entirely (dead code, not imported anywhere)
2. Make supabase-browser.ts createClient() a proper singleton (module-level cache)
3. In UserMenu.tsx, use the singleton client
4. Ensure onAuthStateChange subscription is properly cleaned up on unmount
5. Consider using signOut() utility from supabase-browser.ts instead of window.location.href

## Acceptance Criteria
- No LockManager timeout errors in console
- Header.tsx deleted
- Only one onAuthStateChange listener active at any time
- Sign in/out works without errors
- supabase-browser.ts exports a singleton client
title: Fix Supabase auth LockManager timeout

description:
The map is constrained to the width remaining after the sidebar instead of being full-screen. This needs a layout overhaul:

## Current (broken)
Map and sidebar sit side-by-side in a flex row. Map width = page width - sidebar width.

## Desired layout

### Map (always)
- Full screen, edge to edge horizontally
- Vertically: from bottom of header to top of footer
- Position absolute/fixed, z-index below sidebar

### Sidebar — responsive overlay
- **Desktop (wide viewport)**: Left-side overlay panel on top of the map, between header and footer. Semi-transparent background so map is visible behind it. Does NOT push the map.
- **Mobile (narrow viewport)**: Bottom sheet overlay. Lower edge sits at top of footer, extends upward taking a portion of the vertical space (not full height — leave map visible above). Can be scrolled for venue list.

### Breakpoint
- Use a reasonable breakpoint (e.g. 768px) to switch between left-side overlay and bottom sheet

## Key files
- src/components/HappyHourApp.tsx (main layout container with flex row)
- src/components/MapView.tsx (map component — needs to fill entire viewport area)
- src/components/Sidebar.tsx (needs position: fixed/absolute as overlay)
- src/app/globals.css (layout styles)

## Important
- Do NOT change the sidebar content/functionality, only its positioning
- Map must receive click events even when sidebar is partially visible (pointer-events: none on overlay container, pointer-events: auto on sidebar content)
- Sidebar should have pointer-events so it is still scrollable/clickable
- Header and footer z-index must be above both map and sidebar
title: Fix layout: map full-screen, sidebar as bottom overlay

description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-tww2
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T19:44:41Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
When selecting a venue from the map, the sidebar should scroll to show the venue card. Currently fails because: (1) no scroll-padding-top for sticky neighborhood headers, (2) scrollIntoView fires before DOM expansion completes, (3) block:"nearest" insufficient when card is in collapsed section.

## Key Files
- src/components/Sidebar.tsx — VenueList, scroll-to-card logic, sticky headers
- src/components/BottomSheet.tsx — also contains VenueList, same scroll issues
- src/app/globals.css — .neighborhood-header sticky, .sidebar-scroll

## Fix Approach
1. Add scroll-padding-top to account for sticky neighborhood headers
2. Delay scrollIntoView until after neighborhood expansion animation completes
3. Use block:"center" or block:"start" instead of "nearest"
4. Ensure BottomSheet auto-expands to "half" BEFORE scrollIntoView fires
5. Reference working CRA version: commit ed5ebc856051f9340e7229a72fd06da9194bca64

## Acceptance Criteria
- Click map pin → sidebar scrolls to card, card fully visible (not behind sticky header)
- Click map pin in collapsed neighborhood → neighborhood expands THEN scrolls to card
- On mobile, bottom sheet expands to half then scrolls to card
title: Fix sidebar scroll-to-card on venue selection

description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-9m9i
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T21:01:00Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
No E2E tests exist. Need comprehensive Playwright test suite covering the UI in all responsive modes after the bug fixes are complete.

## Viewport Projects
- Desktop (1280x720+) — sidebar + map side-by-side layout
- Mobile portrait (375x812, iPhone-style) — bottom sheet + map, no sidebar
- Mobile landscape (812x375) — compact layout with bottom sheet
- Tablet (768x1024) — verify breakpoint behavior

## Test Cases Required
1. Page load: all pins rendered on map, venue count matches sidebar/bottom sheet
2. Day filter: clicking filter updates venue list AND map pin visibility
3. Neighborhood expand/collapse: sidebar headers work, map zooms to neighborhood
4. Venue selection: click card → map centers on pin, info window opens, card highlighted
5. Venue selection from map: click pin → sidebar/bottom sheet scrolls to card, card highlighted
6. Rapid clicking: click 10+ venues quickly → no pins disappear, no console errors
7. Bottom sheet drag: peek → half → full snap points work on mobile
8. Info window: displays restaurant name, deal, favicon, links (Site/Go)
9. Auth flow: sign in/out does not cause LockManager errors (check console)
10. Responsive transitions: resize window across breakpoints, layout adapts correctly

## Setup
- Use @playwright/test with projects for each viewport
- Mock Supabase auth for consistent test behavior
- Mock Google Maps API or use deterministic stub
- playwright.config.ts with webServer pointing to npm run dev
- Tests run in CI (GitHub Actions) and locally via npx playwright test

## Acceptance Criteria
- playwright.config.ts with 4 viewport projects
- All 10 test cases passing across all viewports where applicable
- CI integration via GitHub Actions workflow
- npx playwright test works locally
title: Add Playwright E2E test suite for all responsive modes

User-submitted photos (menus, signage) are sent to Claude for deal extraction but never persisted. Store them for future rich content.

## Changes

### 1. Create storage + table (supabase migration)
- venue_photos table: id, venue_id (FK→venues), storage_path, media_type, uploaded_by (FK→auth.users), created_at
- RLS: public read, authenticated write
- Supabase Storage bucket 'venue-photos'
- Migration file: supabase/migrations/20260304_003_venue_photos.sql

### 2. Upload photos during venue save (venues/route.ts)
- Accept images array (base64 + mediaType) in POST body
- After saving venue row, upload each to Storage (venue-photos/{venue_id}/{uuid}.{ext})
- Insert rows into venue_photos

### 3. Pass images from client (DealUpdaterApp.tsx)
- Include base64 images in save request (already in state from extract step)

## Key files
- supabase/migrations/20260304_003_venue_photos.sql (NEW)
- src/app/api/venues/route.ts — photo upload after venue save
- src/components/deal-updater/DealUpdaterApp.tsx — pass images to save
title: Photo collection: store user-submitted photos in Supabase Storage

## DB Migration
supabase/migrations/20260304_004_venue_pin_fields.sql:

### Fix 3: Geocoding Logging

attached_molecule: ha-wisp-mu50
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T19:44:11Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
fitBounds and panTo effects race when selecting a venue also sets selectedNeighborhood. In HappyHourApp.tsx handleVenueSelect (line ~80-82), selecting a venue from a different neighborhood sets BOTH selectedVenue AND selectedNeighborhood. This triggers both the panTo effect and the fitBounds effect in the same render cycle. fitBounds wins and overrides the pan-to-venue.

## Key Files
- src/components/MapView.tsx — fitBounds effect, panTo effect
- src/components/HappyHourApp.tsx — handleVenueSelect sets both states

## Fix Approach
1. Separate the venue-selection and neighborhood-selection concerns
2. When a venue is selected, panTo should take priority over fitBounds
3. Consider: only fitBounds when neighborhood is selected WITHOUT a venue
4. Reference working CRA version: commit ed5ebc856051f9340e7229a72fd06da9194bca64

## Acceptance Criteria
- Select venue from different neighborhood → map pans to venue, not zooms to neighborhood
- Click neighborhood header → map zooms to fit all neighborhood venues
- Deselect venue → map stays at current zoom level
title: Fix neighborhood zoom race condition (fitBounds vs panTo)

attached_molecule: ha-wisp-w90l
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T19:47:45Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
Google S2 favicon service (google.com/s2/favicons) is unreliable — returns 404s or generic globe icons for many restaurant domains. Every VenueCard fires an external request to Google on render. With 50+ venues, that is 50+ third-party image requests at page load, many of which fail silently.

## Key Files
- src/components/VenueCard.tsx — favicon URL computation, img element
- src/components/MapView.tsx — info window also needs favicons

## Fix Approach
1. Add proper fallback: if favicon fails to load, show a styled first-letter avatar
2. Add onError handler to img to show fallback
3. Add loading="lazy" to favicon images
4. Optionally: use a more reliable favicon service or cache to Supabase Storage

## Acceptance Criteria
- No 404 errors for favicon requests in console
- Fallback displayed when favicon unavailable (not broken image icon)
- Favicons load lazily (not blocking initial render)
title: Fix favicon 404s — add fallback and lazy loading

dispatched_by: mayor

After adding/updating a deal via Deal Updater, data doesn't appear on the main page until a new deployment or manual refresh. Root cause: Next.js caches the home page indefinitely with no revalidation.

## Required Changes

### 1. Add revalidatePath after venue writes
- src/app/api/venues/route.ts: After successful INSERT/UPDATE, call revalidatePath('/') to bust the SSR cache
- Import revalidatePath from 'next/cache'

### 2. Add ISR to home page  
- src/app/page.tsx: Add 'export const revalidate = 60' for 60-second incremental static regeneration as a safety net

### 3. Add Cache-Control headers to GET /api/venues
- Return with 'Cache-Control: no-cache' header so Deal Updater always gets fresh venue list

### 4. Add dynamic directive to home page
- src/app/page.tsx: Add 'export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"' so the page always fetches fresh data on each request (not just every 60s)

## Key Files
- src/app/api/venues/route.ts (lines 38-143 POST handler, lines 7-36 GET handler)
- src/app/page.tsx (server component that calls getVenues())
- src/lib/venues.ts (getVenues -> fetchFromSupabase)
title: Fix stale data: add cache revalidation after venue writes + ISR on home page

AI extracts inconsistent neighborhood names (e.g. 'Midtown Atlanta' vs 'Midtown') creating duplicate groups in the sidebar.

## Fix 1: Tell Claude about existing neighborhoods (extract-deal/route.ts)
- Extract distinct neighborhoods from the venues list already in the request payload
- Add to prompt: 'Existing neighborhoods: [list]. ALWAYS use existing name if restaurant is in/near that area. Only create new if none apply. Use shortest common name.'

## Fix 2: Normalize on save — safety net (venues/route.ts)
- After determining neighborhood string, before saving:
  1. Query distinct neighborhoods from venues table
  2. Fuzzy match: normalize both (lowercase, strip trailing ' atlanta', trim)
  3. Match found → use existing canonical name
  4. No match → save as-is

## Key files
- src/app/api/extract-deal/route.ts — neighborhood list in prompt
- src/app/api/venues/route.ts — neighborhood normalization before INSERT/UPDATE
title: Neighborhood normalization: prompt hint + fuzzy match on save

description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-mmq1
attached_at: 2026-02-23T19:27:05Z
dispatched_by: mayor

## Problem
The Deal Updater submit button does nothing — handleSubmit() in src/components/deal-updater/DealUpdaterApp.tsx (line ~203) just calls setView(success) then resetApp(). No data is ever saved to Supabase. Also, lat/lng, restaurant_url, and maps_url are never populated (no geocoding).

## Required Changes

### 1. Add POST /api/venues/route.ts
Add a POST handler alongside the existing GET. It should:
- Require authenticated user (via supabase-server createClient)
- Accept body: { extractedData: ExtractedDeal, matchedVenueId?: string | null }
- Geocode the restaurant using Google Places API (Text Search then Place Details):
  - Text Search: GET https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/textsearch/json?query={restaurant_name}+Atlanta&key=NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
  - Place Details: GET https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/details/json?place_id={placeId}&fields=geometry,website,formatted_address,address_components,url&key=NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
  - Extract: lat, lng, website (restaurant_url), result.url (maps_url), neighborhood from address_components (type neighborhood > sublocality_level_1 > extractedData.google_place.neighborhood fallback)
  - If geocoding fails, fall back gracefully (null lat/lng, no URL)
- Build venue row: { restaurant_name, deal: deal_description, neighborhood, latitude, longitude, restaurant_url, maps_url, mon, tue, wed, thu, fri }
  - days mapping: monday->mon, tuesday->tue, wednesday->wed, thursday->thu, friday->fri
- If matchedVenueId provided: UPDATE venues SET ... WHERE id = matchedVenueId
- If new: INSERT INTO venues (...)
- Use supabase admin client (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) for the write to bypass RLS
- Return { success: true, venue: savedRow }

### 2. Check src/lib/supabase-server.ts
If there is no createAdminClient, add one that uses createClient from @supabase/supabase-js with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY.

### 3. Wire handleSubmit in DealUpdaterApp.tsx
Replace current handleSubmit (line ~203-206) to:
- Set loading state (add isSubmitting state)
- POST /api/venues with { extractedData, matchedVenueId: matchedEntry?.id ?? null }
- On success: setView(success) then setTimeout(resetApp, 3000)
- On error: setError(message), stay on result view
- Disable the submit button while isSubmitting

## Notes
- The venues table only has mon/tue/wed/thu/fri columns, no sat/sun
- Work on a feature branch, push, run gt done
title: Fix Deal Updater: wire submit to save venues + add Google Places geocoding

description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-xqty
attached_at: 2026-02-23T16:39:27Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Replace the broken word-tokenization matching in DealUpdaterApp.tsx with a full semantic + location-aware matching system.

## What to build

### 1. Photo GPS extraction (client-side)
Install `exifr` (npm install exifr) — lightweight browser-compatible EXIF parser.
When a photo is selected in handleImageCapture(), extract GPS coordinates:
```ts
import exifr from "exifr"
const gps = await exifr.gps(file)  // returns { latitude, longitude } or undefined
```
Store the best GPS fix from any of the uploaded images.

### 2. User geolocation (client-side)
Add a `userLocation` state to DealUpdaterApp. On component mount, call:
```ts
if ("geolocation" in navigator) {
  navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(
    (pos) => setUserLocation({ lat: pos.coords.latitude, lng: pos.coords.longitude, source: "gps" }),
    () => {} // permission denied — silent fail, not required
  )
}
```
This prompts the user for location permission. The browser shows its native permission dialog — no custom UI needed.
Location source priority: EXIF GPS > user geolocation > null.

### 3. Server-side AI semantic matching — extend /api/extract-deal
Update the extract-deal route to:
- Accept `venues` (array of {id, restaurant_name, neighborhood, latitude?, longitude?}) in request body
- Accept `location` ({lat, lng, source}) in request body
- Include both in the Claude prompt so Claude performs semantic matching in one pass

Update the Claude prompt to append:
```
You also need to identify if this restaurant already exists in our database.

User location: ${location ? `${location.lat}, ${location.lng} (from ${location.source})` : "unknown"}

Existing venues (JSON):
${JSON.stringify(venues.map(v => ({id: v.id, name: v.restaurant_name, neighborhood: v.neighborhood, lat: v.latitude, lng: v.longitude})))}

Add a "matched_venue_id" field to your JSON response:
- Set it to the numeric id of the best matching existing venue if confidence is high (same restaurant, possibly different name format)
- Set it to null if this appears to be a new restaurant not in the list
- Use the name semantics AND proximity (if location available) to determine match
- Do NOT match on superficial word overlap — only match if you are confident it is the same physical restaurant
```

Update the response type to include `matched_venue_id: number | null`.

### 4. Remove client-side word matching
In DealUpdaterApp.tsx:
- Remove the `existingEntries.find()` block (lines ~135-139)
- Remove the `existingEntries` state and the fetch("/api/venues") call on mount
- Instead, pass `existingEntries` to the API call at extraction time (fetch them right before calling extract-deal, or keep them loaded but pass to API)
- Set `matchedEntry` from `existingEntries.find(v => v.id === extracted.matched_venue_id)` after getting API response

### 5. Update TypeScript types
In src/lib/deal-types.ts, add `matched_venue_id?: number | null` to ExtractedDeal type.

## Notes
- exifr is ~20KB gzipped, tree-shakeable, works in Next.js client components
- Only JPEG/HEIC/TIFF photos have EXIF GPS; screenshots/PNGs will return undefined — handle gracefully
- The venues list is passed per-request (not cached in API) since it may be 100-200 items — fine for a Claude prompt
- Do NOT build a custom embedding/vector search system — Claude handles semantic matching in context
title: Semantic AI matching with GPS: replace word matching in Deal Updater

description:
Add GPS-based locate-me to the map with silent geolocation on load, GPS button with acquisition animation, ME beacon, and distance-sorted venues.

## Geolocation State (HappyHourApp.tsx)
- New state: userLocation, gpsState (idle/acquiring/located/denied)
- On mount: silently call navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition
- Pass userLocation and gpsState to MapView and BottomSheet

## GPS Button (MapView.tsx)
- Position: bottom-right, absolute
- Idle: 56px white circle, brand-purple border, crosshair icon, 'locate me' label
- Acquiring: purple→orange gradient fill, white icon spins, 'finding...' label
- Located: returns to idle visual
- Denied: stays idle forever
- On tap: getCurrentPosition, animate to user location

## ME Beacon (MapView.tsx)
- AdvancedMarker at user coords, z-index above venue pins
- 46px circle, purple→orange gradient, 🙋 emoji centered
- Gentle pulse: scale 100%→112% loop
- Two expanding radar rings (purple + orange), staggered fade
- 'ME' pill label below, non-interactive

## Map Animation (MapView.tsx)
- On location acquired: map.panTo + map.setZoom(14) smooth animation

## Distance Sorting (HappyHourApp.tsx)
- Sort filteredVenues by haversine distance from user
- Closer venues get higher z-index on map

## Distance in Venue Card (VenueCard.tsx)
- Accept optional distance prop, show '0.4 mi away' below neighborhood

## Count Strip (BottomSheet.tsx)
- Located: '6 deals near you' instead of '6 spots'

## New Utility
- src/lib/geo.ts (NEW): haversineDistance(lat1, lng1, lat2, lng2) returns miles
title: Locate-me: GPS button, ME beacon, distance sorting

close_reason: Merged to main at 49469ee — Supabase auth complete
description:
attached_molecule: ha-wisp-tgg8
attached_at: 2026-02-23T13:29:13Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Replace the shared password gate (DEAL_UPDATER_PASSWORD) with real Supabase email/password authentication. Add an admin page for user management and invites. All changes in ONE feature branch.

## Environment Variables Needed (already have NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (for admin operations — server-side only)
- Remove DEAL_UPDATER_PASSWORD after migration

## Phase 1: Install deps + create Supabase clients

### 1a. Install @supabase/ssr
```bash
npm install @supabase/ssr
```

### 1b. NEW FILE: src/lib/supabase-browser.ts
Browser client using createBrowserClient from @supabase/ssr:
```ts
import { createBrowserClient } from "@supabase/ssr";

export function createClient() {
  return createBrowserClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
  );
}
```

### 1c. NEW FILE: src/lib/supabase-server.ts
Server client using createServerClient from @supabase/ssr. Uses next/headers cookies():
```ts
import { createServerClient } from "@supabase/ssr";
import { cookies } from "next/headers";

export async function createClient() {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  return createServerClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    {
      cookies: {
        getAll() { return cookieStore.getAll(); },
        setAll(cookiesToSet) {
          cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) => {
            try { cookieStore.set(name, value, options); } catch { /* server component */ }
          });
        },
      },
    }
  );
}
```

### 1d. NEW FILE: src/lib/supabase-admin.ts
Admin client with service role key (server-only):
```ts
import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";

export function createAdminClient() {
  return createClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!,
    { auth: { autoRefreshToken: false, persistSession: false } }
  );
}
```

### 1e. Keep existing src/lib/supabase.ts
venues.ts imports it — keep for backwards compat. No changes needed.

## Phase 2: Middleware for session refresh + route protection

### NEW FILE: src/middleware.ts (project root of src/)
Standard Supabase SSR middleware pattern:
- Refreshes auth session on every request using createServerClient from @supabase/ssr
- Route protection:
  - /deal-updater/* → requires authenticated user (redirect to /login if not)
  - /admin/* → requires authenticated user with role=admin (check profiles table)
  - /api/extract-deal, /api/enhance-deal → requires auth (return 401)
  - Everything else (/, /api/venues) → public
- For admin role check in middleware: after getting user, query profiles table for role
- Export config matcher: ["/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)"]

## Phase 3: Database migration

### NEW FILE: supabase/migrations/20260223_002_auth_profiles.sql
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS profiles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  email TEXT NOT NULL,
  display_name TEXT,
  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, email, role)
  VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.email, COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'role', 'member'));
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION handle_new_user();

ALTER TABLE profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "profiles_self_read" ON profiles
  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id);

CREATE POLICY "profiles_admin_all" ON profiles
  FOR ALL USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'));

CREATE TRIGGER profiles_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON profiles
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
```

IMPORTANT: Apply this migration with `supabase db push` as part of this work.

## Phase 4: Auth pages

### 4a. NEW FILE: src/app/login/page.tsx
"use client" page. Unicorn-themed matching existing deal-updater design:
- Email + password form (gradient background from-pink-200 via-purple-200 to-blue-200)
- Uses supabase-browser client: supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })
- On success: router.push("/deal-updater")
- Error handling for invalid credentials
- Link back to home

### 4b. NEW FILE: src/app/auth/confirm/page.tsx
"use client" page for handling Supabase invite email redirects:
- Supabase invite emails redirect with hash fragment containing access_token, type=invite
- On mount, check hash params — if type=invite or type=recovery, exchange token
- Use supabase.auth.getSession() after redirect to verify user is authed
- Show password-set form: call supabase.auth.updateUser({ password })
- On success redirect to /deal-updater

### 4c. MODIFY: src/app/deal-updater/page.tsx
Remove the entire password gate. Replace with server-side auth check:
- Convert to server component (remove "use client")
- Import createClient from supabase-server
- const supabase = await createClient(); const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
- If no user → redirect("/login") (from next/navigation)
- If user → render <DealUpdaterApp />

### 4d. MODIFY: src/components/Header.tsx
Make auth-aware. Since Header is client component:
- Add state for user (check supabase-browser client on mount: supabase.auth.getUser())
- If logged in: show user email/initial + "Sign Out" button (calls supabase.auth.signOut())
- If not logged in: show "Sign In" link to /login (keep unicorn icon)
- If user has admin role: show "Admin" link (need to fetch profile for role)

## Phase 5: Admin page

### 5a. NEW FILE: src/app/admin/page.tsx
"use client" page (protected by middleware for admin role).

Members list section:
- Fetches GET /api/admin/users
- Table: email, display_name, role, created_at
- Role dropdown per user → PATCH /api/admin/users with { userId, role }
- Deactivate button → DELETE /api/admin/users/[id]

Invite section:
- Email input + role selector (admin/member)
- Submit → POST /api/admin/invite
- Show pending invites if available
- Unicorn-themed styling matching the rest of the app

### 5b. NEW FILE: src/app/api/admin/users/route.ts
- GET handler: verify auth + admin role, return all profiles
- PATCH handler: verify auth + admin role, update profile role
  ```ts
  const supabase = await createClient(); // from supabase-server
  const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
  if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
  // Check admin role
  const { data: profile } = await supabase.from("profiles").select("role").eq("id", user.id).single();
  if (profile?.role !== "admin") return NextResponse.json({ error: "Forbidden" }, { status: 403 });
  ```

### 5c. NEW FILE: src/app/api/admin/invite/route.ts
- POST handler: verify admin, call createAdminClient().auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail(email, { data: { role }, redirectTo: siteUrl + "/auth/confirm" })

### 5d. NEW FILE: src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/route.ts
- DELETE handler: verify admin, call createAdminClient().auth.admin.deleteUser(id)

## Phase 6: Protect existing API routes

### MODIFY: src/app/api/extract-deal/route.ts
Replace cookie check (lines 7-9) with Supabase auth:
```ts
import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase-server";
// ...
const supabase = await createClient();
const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
```

### MODIFY: src/app/api/enhance-deal/route.ts
Same replacement as extract-deal.

### DELETE: src/app/api/auth/deal-updater/route.ts
Remove the old shared password auth endpoint entirely. Delete the whole directory.

## Verification Checklist
1. npm run build must pass clean (no type errors)
2. Migration applied via supabase db push
3. Test: visit /deal-updater unauthenticated → redirected to /login
4. Test: sign in → redirected back to /deal-updater, can use app
5. Test: /admin blocked for non-admin users
6. Test: API routes return 401 without auth

## WORKFLOW REQUIREMENTS
- Work on a FEATURE BRANCH (never push to main directly)
- Run: npm run build (must pass clean)
- When done: push feature branch, run gt done

description:
Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Ephemeral Polecat Model

Polecats are truly ephemeral - done at MR submission, recyclable immediately:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → nuked
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat's branch is pushed (cleanup_status=clean), the polecat can be
nuked immediately. The MR continues independently in the Refinery. If conflicts
arise, Refinery creates a NEW conflict-resolution task for a NEW polecat.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, don't maintain state
- **Events over state**: POLECAT_DONE mail triggers immediate cleanup
- **Ephemeral by default**: Clean polecats are nuked immediately, no waiting
- **Cleanup wisps for exceptions**: Only created when intervention needed
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).
title: mol-witness-patrol

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd mol current` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `gt hook` to find your pinned molecule, then `bd show`
for step details — do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command (default: `go test ./...`) |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.
title: mol-refinery-patrol

description:
Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
# After each logical unit of work:
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ha-rwuyt)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work:**
If you find bugs or improvements outside your scope:
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
# Note the ID, continue with your work
```

Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck:**
Don't spin for more than 15 minutes. Mail Witness:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ha-rwuyt
Trying to: <what you're attempting>
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.
title: Implement the solution

description:
Submit your work and clean up. You cease to exist after this step.

**Self-Cleaning Model:**
Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
1. Pushes your branch to origin
2. Creates an MR bead in the merge queue
3. Nukes your sandbox (worktree removal)
4. Exits your session immediately

**Run gt done:**
```bash
gt done
```

You should see output like:
```
✓ Work submitted to merge queue
  MR ID: gt-xxxxx
  Source: polecat/<name>
  Target: main
  Issue: ha-rwuyt
✓ Sandbox nuked
✓ Session exiting
```

**What happens next (not your concern):**
- Refinery processes your MR from the queue
- Refinery rebases and merges to main
- Refinery closes the issue
- If conflicts: Refinery spawns a FRESH polecat to re-implement

You are NOT involved in any of that. You're gone. Done means gone.

**Exit criteria:** Work submitted, sandbox nuked, session exited.
title: Submit work and self-clean

description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gt prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
gt hook               # Shows your pinned molecule and hook_bead
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ha-jywi6           # Full issue details
```

**3. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gt mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages
```

**4. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?

**5. Verify you can proceed:**
- No unresolved blockers on the issue
- You understand what to do
- Required resources are available

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness immediately:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ha-jywi6
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin implementation.
title: Load context and verify assignment

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd mol current` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `gt hook` to find your pinned molecule, then `bd show`
for step details — do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command (default: `go test ./...`) |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**The Scotty Principle:** Don't walk past a broken warp core. But also don't
let someone else's mess consume your entire mission.

**1. Run pre-flights on main:**

Your branch was just created from or rebased on `origin/main` with no
implementation changes yet — you're already at the base branch state.

Run each configured check, then tests:

If typecheck_command is set: ``
If lint_command is set: ``
If test_command is set: `go test ./...`

```bash
       # Check for type errors (if command set)
            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
go test ./...            # Run tests (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured" — skip silently.

**2. If pre-flights pass:**

Continue to implement step.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures yourself — that is
not your assignment. Your job is to fix the issue on your hook, not main.

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Any pre-existing failure | File bead, proceed with your work |

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.
You work on YOUR feature branch only. `gt done` handles push/MR.

**File and proceed path:**
```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1

gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Found pre-existing failures on main.
Filed: <bead-id>
Proceeding with my assigned work (ha-pzjmh)."
```

**Context consideration:**
If investigating pre-existing failures consumed significant context:
```bash
gt handoff -s "Investigated pre-existing failures, ready for assigned work" -m "Issue: ha-pzjmh
Found: <what failed>
Ready to start: implement step"
```
Fresh session continues from implement.

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass on main (or issue filed), ready to implement.
title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Ensure you're on a clean feature branch ready for work.

**1. Check current branch state:**
```bash
git status
git branch --show-current
```

**2. If not on a feature branch, create one:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout -b polecat/<name> origin/main
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Should be empty
```

If dirty state from previous work:
```bash
# If changes are relevant to this issue:
git add -A && git commit -m "WIP: <description>"

# If changes are unrelated cruft:
git stash push -m "unrelated changes before ha-pzjmh"
# Or discard if truly garbage:
git checkout -- .
```

**4. Sync with main:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main      # Get latest, rebase your branch
```

If rebase conflicts:
- Resolve them carefully
- Test after resolution
- If stuck, mail Witness

**5. Run project setup (if configured):**

If setup_command is set, run it to install dependencies:
```bash

```

This ensures dependencies are installed before pre-flight checks.
Empty setup_command means "not configured" — skip this step.

**Exit criteria:** You're on a clean feature branch, rebased on latest main, dependencies installed.
title: Set up working branch

completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Ephemeral Polecat Model

Polecats are truly ephemeral - done at MR submission, recyclable immediately:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → nuked
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat's branch is pushed (cleanup_status=clean), the polecat can be
nuked immediately. The MR continues independently in the Refinery. If conflicts
arise, Refinery creates a NEW conflict-resolution task for a NEW polecat.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, don't maintain state
- **Events over state**: POLECAT_DONE mail triggers immediate cleanup
- **Ephemeral by default**: Clean polecats are nuked immediately, no waiting
- **Cleanup wisps for exceptions**: Only created when intervention needed
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Submit your work and clean up. You cease to exist after this step.

**Self-Cleaning Model:**
Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
1. Pushes your branch to origin
2. Creates an MR bead in the merge queue
3. Nukes your sandbox (worktree removal)
4. Exits your session immediately

**Run gt done:**
```bash
gt done
```

You should see output like:
```
✓ Work submitted to merge queue
  MR ID: gt-xxxxx
  Source: polecat/<name>
  Target: main
  Issue: ha-auavy
✓ Sandbox nuked
✓ Session exiting
```

**What happens next (not your concern):**
- Refinery processes your MR from the queue
- Refinery rebases and merges to main
- Refinery closes the issue
- If conflicts: Refinery spawns a FRESH polecat to re-implement

You are NOT involved in any of that. You're gone. Done means gone.

**Exit criteria:** Work submitted, sandbox nuked, session exited.
title: Submit work and self-clean

description:
**Config: integration_branch_refinery_enabled = true**
**Config: integration_branch_auto_land = false**

Read the two config values above, then:

- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": Say "Integration branches disabled." Close step.
- If integration_branch_auto_land = "false": Say "Auto-land disabled, nothing to do." Close step.
  FORBIDDEN: If auto_land is false, you MUST NOT land integration branches yourself using
  raw git commands. Do not merge integration branches to the default/target branch. Do not push
  integration branch merges. The auto_land=false setting means landing requires a human
  to run `gt mq integration land` manually. Respect this boundary unconditionally.
- If BOTH are "true":
  1. `bd list --type=epic --status=open` to find epics
  2. `gt mq integration status <epic-id>` for each epic
  3. If `ready_to_land: true`: run `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`
  4. If `ready_to_land: false`: do nothing, epic work is incomplete
  Never land partial epics — ALL children must be closed first.
title: Check integration branches for landing

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**The Scotty Principle:** Don't walk past a broken warp core. But also don't
let someone else's mess consume your entire mission.

**1. Run pre-flights on main:**

Your branch was just created from or rebased on `origin/main` with no
implementation changes yet — you're already at the base branch state.

Run each configured check, then tests:

If typecheck_command is set: ``
If lint_command is set: ``
If test_command is set: `go test ./...`

```bash
       # Check for type errors (if command set)
            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
go test ./...            # Run tests (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured" — skip silently.

**2. If pre-flights pass:**

Continue to implement step.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures yourself — that is
not your assignment. Your job is to fix the issue on your hook, not main.

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Any pre-existing failure | File bead, proceed with your work |

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.
You work on YOUR feature branch only. `gt done` handles push/MR.

**File and proceed path:**
```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1

gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Found pre-existing failures on main.
Filed: <bead-id>
Proceeding with my assigned work (ha-rwuyt)."
```

**Context consideration:**
If investigating pre-existing failures consumed significant context:
```bash
gt handoff -s "Investigated pre-existing failures, ready for assigned work" -m "Issue: ha-rwuyt
Found: <what failed>
Ready to start: implement step"
```
Fresh session continues from implement.

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass on main (or issue filed), ready to implement.
title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch

description:
Ensure ALL implementation work is committed before cleanup.

**CRITICAL: You MUST commit all changes from implementation.**
NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.
ALWAYS commit ALL uncommitted changes from your implementation.

**1. Check for uncommitted changes:**
```bash
git status
```

**2. If there are ANY uncommitted changes, commit them now:**
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <descriptive message> (ha-jywi6)"
```

**3. If working tree is already clean, skip.**

**4. VERIFY:**
```bash
git status
```
Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".

**5. Verify you have commits:**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```
If you made changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.

**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean.
title: Commit all implementation changes

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gt prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
gt hook               # Shows your pinned molecule and hook_bead
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ha-auavy           # Full issue details
```

**3. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gt mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages
```

**4. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?

**5. Verify you can proceed:**
- No unresolved blockers on the issue
- You understand what to do
- Required resources are available

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness immediately:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ha-auavy
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin implementation.
title: Load context and verify assignment

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Ensure you're on a clean feature branch ready for work.

**1. Check current branch state:**
```bash
git status
git branch --show-current
```

**2. If not on a feature branch, create one:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout -b polecat/<name> origin/main
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Should be empty
```

If dirty state from previous work:
```bash
# If changes are relevant to this issue:
git add -A && git commit -m "WIP: <description>"

# If changes are unrelated cruft:
git stash push -m "unrelated changes before ha-jywi6"
# Or discard if truly garbage:
git checkout -- .
```

**4. Sync with main:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main      # Get latest, rebase your branch
```

If rebase conflicts:
- Resolve them carefully
- Test after resolution
- If stuck, mail Witness

**5. Run project setup (if configured):**

If setup_command is set, run it to install dependencies:
```bash

```

This ensures dependencies are installed before pre-flight checks.
Empty setup_command means "not configured" — skip this step.

**Exit criteria:** You're on a clean feature branch, rebased on latest main, dependencies installed.
title: Set up working branch

description:
Verify inbox hygiene before ending patrol cycle.

**Step 1: Check inbox state**
```bash
gt mail inbox
```

In the ephemeral model, most POLECAT_DONE messages are handled immediately
(auto-nuke) and archived. Inbox should contain ONLY:
- Unprocessed messages (just arrived, will handle next cycle)
- MERGED notifications (informational, archive after reading)

**Step 2: Archive any stale messages**

Look for messages that were processed but not archived:
- POLECAT_STARTED older than this cycle → archive
- POLECAT_DONE that was auto-nuked → should be archived already
- MERGED notifications → archive after acknowledging
- HELP/Blocked that was escalated → archive
- SWARM_START that created tracking wisp → archive

```bash
# For each stale message found:
gt mail archive <message-id>
```

**Step 3: Verify cleanup wisp hygiene**

In the ephemeral model, cleanup wisps should be rare (only for dirty polecats):
```bash
bd list --label cleanup --status=open
```

- state:pending → Needs investigation in process-cleanups
- state:merge-requested → Legacy state, handle in inbox-check

If cleanup wisps are accumulating, investigate why polecats aren't clean.

**Goal**: Inbox should be nearly empty. Cleanup wisps should be rare.
title: End-of-cycle inbox hygiene

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Ensure ALL implementation work is committed before cleanup.

**CRITICAL: You MUST commit all changes from implementation.**
NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.
ALWAYS commit ALL uncommitted changes from your implementation.

**1. Check for uncommitted changes:**
```bash
git status
```

**2. If there are ANY uncommitted changes, commit them now:**
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <descriptive message> (ha-rwuyt)"
```

**3. If working tree is already clean, skip.**

**4. VERIFY:**
```bash
git status
```
Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".

**5. Verify you have commits:**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```
If you made changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.

**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean.
title: Commit all implementation changes

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, don't maintain state
- **Events over state**: POLECAT_DONE mail triggers immediate cleanup
- **Ephemeral by default**: Clean polecats are nuked immediately, no waiting
- **Cleanup wisps for exceptions**: Only created when intervention needed
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.
title: mol-refinery-patrol

description:
Ensure workspace is pristine before handoff.

**IMPORTANT: Do NOT run `git push`. That is `gt done`'s job (next step).**
**IMPORTANT: Do NOT discard implementation changes. They must already be committed.**

**1. Check for untracked files:**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Add to .gitignore if appropriate
- Remove if temporary: `rm <file>`
- Commit if needed: `git add <file> && git commit -m "chore: add <file>"`

**2. Check stash:**
```bash
git stash list
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Pop and commit: `git stash pop && git add -A && git commit -m "chore: unstash work (ha-rwuyt)"`
- Or drop if truly garbage: `git stash drop`

**3. Verify clean state and commits:**
```bash
git status                  # Must show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Must be empty
git log origin/main..HEAD   # Your commits (should show your work)
```

If `git log origin/main..HEAD` shows nothing but you DID make changes,
something went wrong. Do NOT proceed — mail Witness for help.

**Exit criteria:** Workspace clean, no cruft, all work committed. Do NOT push — `gt done` handles that.
title: Clean up workspace

title: mol-refinery-patrol

description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd mol current` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `gt hook` to find your pinned molecule, then `bd show`
for step details — do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command (default: `go test ./...`) |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Ensure ALL implementation work is committed before cleanup.

**CRITICAL: You MUST commit all changes from implementation.**
NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.
ALWAYS commit ALL uncommitted changes from your implementation.

**1. Check for uncommitted changes:**
```bash
git status
```

**2. If there are ANY uncommitted changes, commit them now:**
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <descriptive message> (ha-4p1xk)"
```

**3. If working tree is already clean, skip.**

**4. VERIFY:**
```bash
git status
```
Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".

**5. Verify you have commits:**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```
If you made changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.

**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean.
title: Commit all implementation changes

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.

title: mol-witness-patrol

description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
# After each logical unit of work:
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ha-jywi6)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work:**
If you find bugs or improvements outside your scope:
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
# Note the ID, continue with your work
```

Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck:**
Don't spin for more than 15 minutes. Mail Witness:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ha-jywi6
Trying to: <what you're attempting>
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.
